topics: Mesh and node analysis and network theorems
Mesh and node analysis of networks containing independent and dependent sources, supermesh and supernode analysis, steady-state AC analysis using mesh and node analysis, Thevenin's theorem
Maximum power transfer theorem applied to both DC and AC circuits having dependent sources
Transients - DC and sinusoidal response of RL, RC, and RLC circuits
Review of Laplace transforms and inverse Laplace transforms
Transformation of basic signals and circuits into s-domain, transient analysis of RL and RLC networks with impulse, step and sinusoidal inputs (with and without initial conditions)
Transform impedance/admittance of R, L and C, transfer function representation, poles and zeros, analysis of coupled circuits
Dot polarity convention, sinusoidal steady state analysis of coupled circuits, linear transformer as a coupled circuit, analysis of coupled circuits in s-domain
Two port networks and passive filters
Characterization of two port networks using Z, Hybrid, and Transmission parameters
T and π representations, interrelationship among parameter sets, series and parallel connections of two port networks, passive filters - filter fundamentals
Classification of filters, characteristic impedance, design of constant K - low pass, high pass, and band pass filters
